name: Black Bean-Goat Cheese Enchiladas (with Mango Relish)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
To make the sauce, in a saucepan, cook the chicken stock, tomatillos, garlic cloves, onions, and serranos over medium-high heat for 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Transfer the mixture to a blender, add the cilantro, and puree until smooth. Season with salt and set aside.
To make the filling, in another saucepan, bring all the filling ingredients except the cheese and salt to a boil. Remove from the heat, whisk in 4 ounces of goat cheese, and season with salt. Deep warm.
Pour enough vegetable oil in a skillet so it reaches about 1/2 inch up the sides. Heat over medium until just smoking (caution: this is very hot). Submerge each tortilla for 5 seconds on both sides to soften. Drain on paper towels and keep warm; do not stack the tortillas.
To assemble, divide the black bean-goat cheese mixture evenly among the tortillas, spreading down the middle. Roll up seam side down onto a baking sheet or into an ovenproof dish, placing snugly together. Pour reserved sauce over enchiladas and top with remaining 4 ounces of goat cheese. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 10 minutes. Serve 2 enchiladas per plate with mango relish.
